title: Goals, Context, Outputs, Boundaries, and Done Conditions · Mastery Check
summary: Write a production-ready prompt for a high-impact code and document change. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a reusable goal-context-output-boundary template. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State the desired result and audience before prescribing implementation steps.
Provide context that can change the result and route the agent to authoritative sources.
Specify output structure, granularity, evidence, and machine-readable constraints when they matter.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Done Conditions: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed quiz case for prompt prompt anatomy that tests this competency: Define observable completion checks including tests, review, and unresolved-evidence reporting.. steps.why: Retrieving Done Conditions through the concrete a reusable goal-context-output-boundary template case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define observable completion checks including tests, review, and unresolved-evidence reporting.
